annotations.label: Homotopic functions

anchor.snippet: two continuous functions from one topological space to another are called homotopic

annotations.note: `ContinuousMap.Homotopic f₀ f₁` is defined as `Nonempty (ContinuousMap.Homotopy f₀ f₁)`.

annotations.label: Homotopy (formal)

anchor.snippet: Formally, a homotopy between two continuous functions

annotations.note: `ContinuousMap.Homotopy f₀ f₁` is a continuous map `I × X → Y` agreeing with `f₀` at 0 and `f₁` at 1.

annotations.label: Homotopy as family of maps

anchor.snippet: An alternative notation is to say that a homotopy between two continuous functions

annotations.note: `Homotopy.curry` realises a homotopy as a continuous map `C(I, C(X, Y))`, the family-of-maps viewpoint.

annotations.label: Torus to coffee mug homotopy

anchor.snippet: The animation that is looped above right provides an example of a homotopy between two embeddings

annotations.note: No formalization of this specific torus/mug ambient isotopy was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Homotopy is an equivalence relation

anchor.snippet: Being homotopic is an equivalence relation on the set of all continuous functions

annotations.note: `Homotopic.equivalence` packages reflexivity, symmetry and transitivity into an `Equivalence`.

annotations.label: Compatibility with composition

anchor.snippet: This homotopy relation is compatible with function composition

annotations.note: `Homotopic.comp` shows that homotopic pairs compose to homotopic composites.

annotations.label: Simple homotopy between two maps

anchor.snippet: is a homotopy between them

annotations.note: The article's bespoke worked example was not found as a named example in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Linear (straight-line) homotopy

anchor.snippet: then there is a linear homotopy

annotations.note: `Homotopy.affine f g` constructs the straight-line homotopy `(t, x) ↦ AffineMap.lineMap (f x) (g x) t`.

annotations.label: Identity to constant on n-disk

anchor.snippet: be the identity function on the unit

annotations.note: Mathlib proves any convex set (so the closed unit disk) is contractible, which implies the disk's identity is homotopic to a constant, but the specific n-disk example is not isolated.

annotations.label: Homotopy equivalence

anchor.snippet: a homotopy equivalence between X and Y is a pair of continuous maps

annotations.note: `ContinuousMap.HomotopyEquiv X Y` records maps `f, g` with `g ∘ f` and `f ∘ g` homotopic to identities.

annotations.label: Contractible space

anchor.snippet: Spaces that are homotopy equivalent to a point are called contractible

annotations.note: `ContractibleSpace X` is defined as `Nonempty (X ≃ₕ Unit)`.

annotations.label: Homeomorphism implies homotopy equivalence

anchor.snippet: A homeomorphism is a special case of a homotopy equivalence

annotations.note: `Homeomorph.toHomotopyEquiv` upgrades any homeomorphism to a homotopy equivalence.

annotations.label: Disk homotopy equivalent to point

anchor.snippet: A solid disk is homotopy equivalent to a single point

annotations.note: Mathlib shows any (nonempty) convex set is contractible, giving the disk case as an instance, but no specific 'solid disk ≃ point' statement is recorded.

annotations.label: Möbius strip and untwisted strip

anchor.snippet: The Möbius strip and an untwisted (closed) strip are homotopy equivalent

annotations.note: No Möbius-strip homotopy-equivalence result was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euclidean space contractible to point

anchor.snippet: The first example of a homotopy equivalence is

annotations.note: Every real topological vector space (in particular ℝⁿ) is contractible via this instance.

annotations.label: Homotopy equivalence for 1-sphere

anchor.snippet: There is a homotopy equivalence between

annotations.note: No statement that, e.g., the punctured plane is homotopy equivalent to S¹ was located.

annotations.label: Fiber bundle with contractible fiber

anchor.snippet: Any fiber bundle

annotations.note: No result that a fiber bundle with contractible fiber is a homotopy equivalence was found.

annotations.label: Vector bundles are fiber bundles

anchor.snippet: Every vector bundle is a fiber bundle with a fiber homotopy equivalent to a point

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `VectorBundle` extending the fiber-bundle structure, but does not record the homotopy-theoretic fact that its fiber (a real vector space) is contractible as part of the bundle theory.

annotations.label: Contractible subcomplex quotient

anchor.snippet: If a subcomplex

annotations.note: No CW-complex statement about quotienting by a contractible subcomplex was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Deformation retraction is homotopy equivalence

anchor.snippet: A deformation retraction is a homotopy equivalence

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of deformation retract; a grep for `DeformationRetract` returns nothing.

annotations.label: Null-homotopic

anchor.snippet: is said to be null-homotopic if it is homotopic to a constant function

annotations.note: `Nullhomotopic f` is defined as the existence of `y` with `f` homotopic to the constant map at `y`.

annotations.label: Null-homotopic circle maps extend to disk

anchor.snippet: is null-homotopic precisely when it can be continuously extended

annotations.note: The 'null-homotopic ↔ extends to the disk' characterisation was not found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Contractibility via null-homotopic identity

anchor.snippet: is contractible if and only if the identity map

annotations.note: `contractible_iff_id_nullhomotopic` states `ContractibleSpace X ↔ (id : X → X).Nullhomotopic`.

annotations.label: Homotopy invariants of equivalent spaces

anchor.snippet: if X and Y are homotopy equivalent spaces, then

annotations.note: Mathlib transports specific invariants (e.g. simple connectedness) across `≃ₕ`, but does not state a general 'homotopy invariants agree' meta-theorem.

annotations.label: Compactly supported homology is not invariant

anchor.snippet: An example of an algebraic invariant of topological spaces which is not homotopy invariant

annotations.note: Compactly supported homology and this counterexample are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Homotopy relative to a subspace

anchor.snippet: we say that f and g are homotopic relative to K

annotations.note: `HomotopyRel`/`HomotopicRel f₀ f₁ S` define homotopies that are stationary on `S`.

annotations.label: Strong deformation retract

anchor.snippet: this is known as a strong deformation retract

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `(Strong)DeformationRetract` definition.

annotations.label: Pointed homotopy

anchor.snippet: When K is a point, the term pointed homotopy is used

annotations.note: Pointed homotopies arise as `HomotopicRel _ _ {x₀}`, but Mathlib does not introduce a dedicated 'pointed homotopy' abbreviation.

annotations.label: Isotopy

anchor.snippet: This gives rise to the concept of isotopy

annotations.note: No `Isotopy` (homotopy through embeddings/homeomorphisms) is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Reflection is homotopic but not isotopic to identity

anchor.snippet: the map from the interval [−1, 1] into the real numbers defined by

annotations.note: Without an `Isotopy` notion in Mathlib this counterexample is not formalized.

annotations.label: Alexander's trick

anchor.snippet: Two homeomorphisms (which are special cases of embeddings) of the unit ball which agree on the boundary can be shown to be isotopic

annotations.note: Alexander's trick is not formalized; no relevant declaration was found.

annotations.label: Knot equivalence via ambient isotopy

anchor.snippet: Knots K 1 and K 2 are considered equivalent when there is a continuous

annotations.note: Knot theory and ambient isotopy of knots are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Smooth isotopy

anchor.snippet: a path between two smooth embeddings is a smooth isotopy

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of smooth isotopy of embeddings.

annotations.label: Timelike homotopy

anchor.snippet: A timelike homotopy between two timelike curves is a homotopy such that the curve remains timelike

annotations.note: Lorentzian geometry and timelike homotopies are not part of Mathlib.

annotations.label: CTCs not null timelike homotopic

anchor.snippet: No closed timelike curve (CTC) on a Lorentzian manifold is timelike homotopic to a point

annotations.note: No CTC / Lorentzian-manifold theory in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Homotopy lifting property

anchor.snippet: then we can lift all

annotations.note: Mathlib proves the homotopy-lifting property for covering maps via `IsCoveringMap.liftHomotopy`, but does not introduce a general `HomotopyLiftingProperty`/fibration notion.

annotations.label: Homotopy extension property

anchor.snippet: Another useful property involving homotopy is the homotopy extension property

annotations.note: No `HomotopyExtensionProperty`/cofibration definition is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Homotopy groups

anchor.snippet: These groups are called the homotopy groups

annotations.note: `HomotopyGroup N X x` (with the `π_ n` abbreviation) is defined as the quotient of generalised loops by homotopy.

annotations.label: Fundamental group

anchor.snippet: it is also called the fundamental group

annotations.note: `FundamentalGroup X x` is defined as `End (FundamentalGroupoid.mk x)`.

annotations.label: Homotopy category

anchor.snippet: The homotopy category is the category whose objects are topological spaces

annotations.note: Mathlib has `HomotopyCategory` for chain complexes but not the topological homotopy category hTop.

annotations.label: Isomorphism in homotopy category

anchor.snippet: Two topological spaces X and Y are isomorphic in this category if and only if they are homotopy equivalent

annotations.note: Without a topological homotopy category in Mathlib, this characterisation is not stated.

annotations.label: Homology as functorial homotopy invariant

anchor.snippet: homology groups are a functorial homotopy invariant

annotations.note: Singular homology as a functor on the topological homotopy category is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Eilenberg–MacLane representability

anchor.snippet: of based homotopy classes of based maps from X to the Eilenberg–MacLane space

annotations.note: Eilenberg–MacLane spaces and the representability of cohomology by them are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hopf–Whitney theorem

anchor.snippet: homotopy classes between a CW complex and a multiply connected space can be calculated using cohomology as described by the Hopf–Whitney theorem

annotations.note: The Hopf–Whitney theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.
